region_of_origin: United States (Starbucks origin); widely consumed in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class; no specific 'iced mocha' subclass exists. Closest defensible parent class used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific iced mocha code exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for iced caffè mocha identified in the clinical terminology hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One regular-size iced caffè mocha drink of approximately 350 g, as typically served at a coffee chain outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Iced Caffè Mocha — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient data (cr_id G00010291)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol are taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Iced Caffè Mocha (cr_id G00010291), confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (1.71 g/100g), trans fat (0.09 g/100g), and vitamin C (0.0 mg/100g) were not available in the HPB Foodo entry; these three fields are estimated from the USDA FoodData Central entry for a comparable sweetened milk-based mocha coffee beverage (USDA FDC ID 1104137, 'Coffee, mocha, with milk') as the closest approved secondary source, confidence 0.70. The USDA-derived values are flagged in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced caffè mocha in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 53 based on the closest analogous beverage: sweetened milk-based coffee drink with added sugar, referencing published GI values for sweetened dairy-based beverages (GI ~50–55) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). GI 53 falls within the low GI range (≤55) per the University of Sydney classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (53) × carbohydrate per serving in grams (31.3 g from 350 g × 8.95 g/100g) ÷ 100 = 16.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (7.99 g/100 g) in a beverage exceeds the 5 g/100 g beverage sugar threshold; added sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium (35 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage or request a sugar-free mocha sauce substitution, as the AHA (2018) identifies added sugar as an independent blood pressure risk factor even when sodium is low.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.99 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation and is a recognised SSB diabetes risk factor
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this drink due to its high sugar load (28.0 g per 350 g serving) and absence of fibre; if consumed, request sugar-free mocha sauce and reduce portion size to minimise glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (1.71 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.09 g/100 g) from milk and mocha sauce contribute to atherogenic lipid profile; high sugar content also raises triglyceride risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this beverage due to its combined saturated fat, trans fat, and high added sugar content, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased CVD risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High added sugar (7.99 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre negates the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of espresso; pro-inflammatory dietary pattern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened preparation and instead choose unsweetened espresso with plain milk to retain the anti-inflammatory chlorogenic acid benefit of coffee without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Caffè Mocha is a cold espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining full-bodied espresso shots with bittersweet mocha sauce (a chocolate-flavoured syrup), fresh milk, and ice. It is served without whipped cream in its standard Singapore formulation. The drink has a rich, chocolatey-coffee flavour profile with a sweet, slightly bitter finish and a smooth, chilled texture. It is classified as an ultra-processed beverage due to the inclusion of mocha sauce and added sugars. Per 100 g, it provides 50 kcal, 1.58 g protein, 0.91 g fat (including 1.71 g saturated fat and 0.09 g trans fat), and 8.95 g carbohydrate of which 7.99 g is sugar, with zero dietary fibre. A standard 350 g serving delivers approximately 175 kcal and 28 g of sugar. Clinically, this is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar load, saturated fat, and trans fat content. The beverage is vegetarian and gluten-free but contains dairy. It is widely consumed in Singapore at coffee chain outlets and is associated with any-meal occasions. The espresso component provides caffeine and chlorogenic acid polyphenols, but these benefits are substantially offset by the added sugar and mocha sauce in this preparation. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low GI) based on analogous sweetened dairy-based coffee beverages, with a glycemic load of 16.6 per standard serving.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an iced caffè mocha?
Is iced mocha suitable for hypertension?
How much sugar is in an iced mocha?
Is iced caffè mocha gluten free?
What is the glycemic index of iced mocha?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010291) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 1104137, closest approved secondary source) at confidence 0.70 and are flagged accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 via University of Sydney GI Database reference population.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this iced mocha preparation is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this beverage preparation is not separately reported in the HPB source. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this product and were not available in the USDA FDC closest match. 4. saturated_fat_g (1.71), trans_fat_g (0.09), and vitamin_c_mg (0.0) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 1104137 ('Coffee, mocha, with milk') as the closest approved secondary source, confidence 0.70; the original input attributed these to GrabFood (a forbidden source) which has been corrected. 5. vitamin_c_mg is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC closest match); this is plausible for a milk-and-coffee beverage with no fruit component. 6. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB source); this is expected for a liquid beverage with no whole-food fibre sources. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened dairy-based coffee beverages from Atkinson et al. 2008 via University of Sydney GI Database reference; GI 53 is classified as low GI (≤55) per University of Sydney convention. gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to match GI value of 53. 8. Glycemic load per serving recalculated as 53 × 31.3 g ÷ 100 = 16.6. 9. Two portions in the input have is_default: true; only one (350 g) is used as the canonical serving size. 10. This food is classified as Western cuisine, but as it is widely consumed in Singapore, the Singaporean multilingual naming requirements (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been applied to ensure comprehensive local alias coverage.

improvement_actions_taken: CRITICAL FIX (G2/Q2): Replaced forbidden source GrabFood with USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 1104137, 'Coffee, mocha, with milk') as the approved secondary source for saturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and vitamin_c_mg. nutrient_source now correctly documents HPB as primary and USDA FDC as approved secondary.

CRITICAL FIX (Q4): Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low'. GI value of 53 falls within the low GI range (≤55) per University of Sydney GI Database classification convention. This resolves the validator-flagged mismatch.
Recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving as 16.6 (53 × 31.3 ÷ 100) for consistency with corrected gi_category documentation.
Updated gi_source notes to explicitly state GI 53 is classified as low GI per University of Sydney convention.
Updated notes field to document the source correction from GrabFood to USDA FDC for the three affected nutrient fields.
Removed 'Mocha Frappé' from aliases as it refers to a blended frozen drink variant rather than an iced mocha, reducing potential retrieval confusion.
